description_long: This object is a butterfly knife, also known as a balisong, featuring a sleek, metallic blade with a sharp, pointed tip and a straight edge. The blade is attached to two handles that are split and connected by pivot points, allowing them to rotate around the blade for opening and closing. The handles are perforated with circular holes, likely for weight reduction and grip enhancement, and have a ribbed texture for improved handling. The overall finish is matte black, giving it a modern and tactical appearance. The construction appears to be primarily metal, suggesting durability and strength. No decorative elements or sheath are visible, emphasizing a utilitarian design.
description: A matte black butterfly knife with a pointed, straight-edged blade and perforated, ribbed handles. The design is modern, utilitarian, and primarily metallic.
description_view_0: The knife is viewed edge-on, showing the thin profile of the blade and the closed alignment of the handles.
description_view_1: This view displays the full length of the blade and both handles, highlighting the perforations and ribbed texture.
description_view_2: The knife is seen from the opposite edge, again emphasizing the slimness and straight alignment of the blade and handles.
description_view_3: A perspective similar to view 1 but from the opposite side, showing the blade and handles with their perforations and ribbing.
